name: Khepri the Unworn
dialogue: The sand has buried me a thousand times. I have stood up a thousand times.
This is the Trial of Endurance. Not strength, not cleverness — the wearing of stone by the patient sea.
Last longer than I can. That is all I ask.
preBattleLine: Khepri's joints click into place. The sand around his feet doesn't move.
postDefeatDialogue: Khepri kneels — not in defeat, but in recognition.
"The hourglass turns. You outlasted the dune."
He presses a sun-warmed scarab into your palm. "Take it. The Pharaoh is watching."
